stem: Expand \( \left( ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \right)^{[[n]]} \) and write the result in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
solution: Raise to the power (by De Moivre's theorem or direct expansion): \( (([[a]]) + ([[b]])i)^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This raises the conjugate \( a-bi \) to the power instead of \( a+bi \).
This applies the exponent to each part separately, which is not how powers of a sum work.
This raises each term of the binomial to the power separately, dropping all cross terms.
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.

stem: Let \( z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta) \) with \( r = [[r]] \) and \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{6} \). Using De Moivre's theorem, compute \( z^{[[n]]} \) and write the result in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
solution: By De Moivre's theorem, \( z^{[[n]]} = r^{[[n]]}\left(\cos([[n]]\theta) + i\sin([[n]]\theta)\right) \) with \( r^{[[n]]} = ([[r]])^{[[n]]} \) and \( [[n]]\theta = \dfrac{[[n]]\cdot[[k]]\pi}{6} \), giving \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to multiply the angle by \( n \), leaving \( \theta \) unscaled.
This forgets to raise the modulus \( r \) to the power \( n \).
This swaps sine and cosine; the real part must be \( \cos(n\theta) \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.

stem: Let \( z = ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \). Using De Moivre's theorem (\( |z^{n}| = |z|^{n} \)), find \( \left| z^{[[n]]} \right| \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies the modulus by \( n \) instead of raising it to the power \( n \).
This uses \( |z|^{2} = a^{2}+b^{2} \) (forgetting the square root) raised to the \( n \)th power.
This is off by one in the exponent.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This doubles the correct value.

stem: Let \( z = ([[a]]) + ([[b]])i \). Compute \( z^{-[[n]]} \) and write the result as a single simplified expression in the form \( x + yi \).
solution: By De Moivre's theorem in reciprocal form, \( z^{-n} = |z|^{-n}\left(\cos(n\theta) - i\sin(n\theta)\right) \); computing directly, \( \left(([[a]]) + ([[b]])i\right)^{-[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the negative sign on the exponent, computing \( z^{n} \) instead of \( z^{-n} \).
This uses the conjugate \( \bar z \) in place of \( z \) before taking the negative power.
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This both drops the negative exponent and negates the result.
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.
